quote: And then, well . . . might be a spotted spider's bite can kill a lion, but a dragon is a different sort of beast.
why: Dunk implies Targaryen backing for Egg via the hidden signet, revealing his dragon blood to Rohanne without naming him.

quote: Dunk knew he meant Bloodraven. Brynden Rivers was the Hand's true name. His mother had been a Blackwood, his father King Aegon the Fourth.
why: In-text reveal of Lord Bloodraven's birth name and parentage.

quote: He isn't, though. This egg had been hatched of dragons, not of chickens.
why: Narration undercuts Egg's stableboy cover, marking him as dragon-blooded.

quote: He had legitimized the lot upon his deathbed; not only the Great Bastards like Bloodraven, Bittersteel, and Daemon Blackfyre, whose mothers had been ladies, but even the lesser ones he'd fathered on whores and tavern wenches, merchant's daughters, mummer's maidens, and every pretty peasant girl who chanced to catch his eye.
why: Aegon IV's deathbed legitimization of all his bastards, root of the Blackfyre wars.

quote: Young Aemon took up Blackfyre when the blade slipped from his dying father's fingers, so Bloodraven slew him, too, the younger of the twins. Thus perished the black dragon and his sons.
why: Eustace's detailed account of Daemon and his twin sons' deaths on the Redgrass Field.

quote: Our good High Septon was taken, the gods' own voice on earth, with a third of the Most Devout and near all our silent sisters. His Grace King Daeron, sweet Matarys and bold Valarr, the Hand . . . oh, it was a dreadful time.
why: Septon Sefton's account of the Great Spring Sickness's royal and religious toll.

quote: He will not even bestir himself to sire an heir. Queen Aelinor prays daily at the Great Sept, beseeching the Mother Above to bless her with a child, yet she remains a maid.
why: King Aerys I's neglect of siring an heir; Queen Aelinor's unconsummated marriage.

quote: Coldmoat . . . Coldmoat was promised me by Daemon, yes, but . . . it was not for gain, no . . .
why: Eustace's confused half-denial that he rebelled for Coldmoat's sake.

quote: "Trueborn children are made in a marriage bed and blessed by the Father and the Mother, but bastards are born of lust and weakness, he said. King Aegon decreed that his bastards were not bastards, but he could not change their nature. The High Septon said all bastards are born to betrayal
why: The old High Septon's doctrine that bastards, Bloodraven included, are born to betrayal.

quote: As for Aerys, His Grace cares more for old scrolls and dusty prophecies than for lords and laws.
why: King Aerys I's preoccupation with prophecy over governance.

quote: You shall have no hospitality from me. No bread and salt, not even shade and water. You come as an intruder. I forbid you to set foot on Osgrey land.
why: Ser Eustace formally denies guest right to Rohanne's party at the stream boundary.

quote: In the dimness of the lamplit cellar they looked black, but in better light their true color could be seen: deep and dark and purple. Valyrian eyes, thought Dunk. In Westeros, few but the blood of the dragon had eyes that color, or hair that shone like beaten gold and strands of silver woven all together.
why: Egg's shaved head conceals him, but his deep-purple Valyrian eyes betray dragon blood.

quote: Over the arch of the main gate a row of spider banners drooped in the still air, above the older sigil carved deep into the stone. Centuries of wind and weather had worn it down, but the shape of it was still distinct: a rampant lion made of checkered squares.
why: The chequy lion carved above Coldmoat's gate, beneath the newer Webber spider banners -- Osgrey's lost-castle backstory.

quote: "Well," said Egg. "I hear it's tall."
why: Closing line turning Dunk and Egg north toward the Wall instead of south to Summerhall.

quote: How many eyes does Lord Bloodraven have? ran the riddle Egg had heard in Oldtown. A thousand eyes, and one.
why: Introduces the riddle of Bloodraven's all-seeing spy network.

quote: That was before King Aerys had ascended to the Iron Throne and made him the Hand, but even so he cut a striking figure, garbed in smoke and scarlet with Dark Sister on his hip.
why: Bloodraven's appointment as Hand, six years before the novella's present.

quote: He had one eye, and that one red. The other was an empty socket, the gift Bittersteel had given him upon the Redgrass Field.
why: Bloodraven's missing eye, lost to Bittersteel at the Redgrass Field.

quote: Lord Bloodraven had commanded them to return to their own lands and lords, but few obeyed. Many blamed Bloodraven and King Aerys for the drought. It was a judgment from the gods, they said, for the kinslayer is accursed.
why: Smallfolk blame the drought on Bloodraven as a kinslayer's curse.

quote: He never sweated much. He liked the heat. In Dorne he went about bare-chested, and turned brown as a Dornishman. It is his dragon blood, Dunk told himself.
why: Dunk privately attributes Egg's heat tolerance to his hidden dragon blood.

quote: The Red Widow, she was called, for the husbands she had put into the ground. Old Sam Stoops said she was a witch, a poisoner, and worse.
why: Rohanne's fearsome local reputation as the Red Widow, witch and poisoner.

quote: When you accept a lord's meat and mead, all you do reflects on him, Ser Arlan used to say. Always do more than he expects of you, never less. Never flinch at any task or hardship. And above all, never shame the lord you serve. At Standfast, "meat and mead" meant chicken and ale, but Ser Eustace ate the same plain fare himself.
why: Sworn-sword's plain board at Standfast; Eustace shares his men's fare rather than living apart.

quote: If he had an inkling who you were, he'd piss himself before he laid a finger on you.
why: Dunk's private thought guarding Egg's concealed highborn/dragon identity from Bennis.

quote: how Queen Alysanne convinced King Jaehaerys to abolish the first night
why: Egg cites Alysanne's persuasion of Jaehaerys I to abolish the lord's right of the first night.

quote: The boys were Eustace Osgrey's sons: Edwyn, Harrold, Addam. Edwyn and Harrold had been knights, Addam a young squire. They had died on the Redgrass Field fifteen years ago, at the end of the Blackfyre Rebellion.
why: Eustace's three sons, their deaths on the Redgrass Field ending the Blackfyre Rebellion.

quote: "The Little Lion's shield." The old man rubbed at the rim, and some flakes of rust came off. "Ser Wilbert Osgrey bore this at the battle where he died. I am sure you know the tale."
why: Introduces the Little Lion's shield, relic of Ser Wilbert Osgrey's death in battle.

quote: Dunk was known in the villages, if only as Ser Eustace's new knight, but not so much as a cup of water was offered him.
why: Osgrey smallfolk withhold even water hospitality from their own lord's new knight.

quote: The woman has a spider's heart. She murdered three of her husbands. And all her brothers died in swaddling clothes.
why: Eustace's account of Rohanne's reputation as a husband-killer.

quote: Aegon of House Targaryen was the fourth and youngest son of Maekar, Prince of Summerhall, himself the fourth son of the late King Daeron the Good, the Second of His Name, who'd sat the Iron Throne for five-and-twenty years until the Great Spring Sickness took him off.
why: Full in-text genealogical reveal of Egg's true identity as Prince Aegon Targaryen.

quote: "To keep my hair shaved or dyed, and tell no man my true name," the boy said, with obvious reluctance.
why: Maekar's standing order to Egg, the concealment mechanic running through the novellas.

quote: And once my sister Rhae put a love potion in my drink, so I'd marry her instead of my sister Daella.
why: Egg recounts his sister Rhae's attempt to secure a Targaryen incestuous match.

quote: How many eyes does Lord Bloodraven have? A thousand eyes, and one.
why: The riddle recurs as Dunk questions Egg about guarding his identity.

quote: Lady Shiera does. Lord Bloodraven's paramour. She bathes in blood to keep her beauty.
why: Egg's gossip about Shiera Seastar, Bloodraven's paramour, and her blood-bathing beauty legend.

quote: Ser Arlan had died of a chill, Prince Baelor of the blow his brother dealt him during Dunk's trial of seven, his son Valarr during the Great Spring Sickness.
why: Baelor Breakspear's death from Maekar's blow at Dunk's trial of seven.

quote: 'Twas Aegon's son King Maegor who took Coldmoat from us, when Lord Ormond Osgrey spoke out against his supression of the Stars and Swords, as the Poor Fellows and the Warrior's Sons were called.
why: King Maegor I's seizure of Coldmoat from House Osgrey over Ormond Osgrey's opposition to his suppression of the Faith militant.

quote: "You'd best not take any food or drink at Coldmoat, ser. The Red Widow poisoned all her husbands."
why: Egg's guest-right/poison warning before Dunk's embassy to Coldmoat.

quote: It comes to me that in days gone by, when the green kings ruled, you could pay a man a blood price if you had slain one of his animals or peasants.
why: Eustace proposes an archaic green-kings-era blood price to settle the dam dispute.

quote: Eight for them and one for me and one for old Ser Useless, and one last one for your bald-head boy.
why: Dunk's nightmare names a grave for Egg among the doomed Standfast recruits.

quote: Since the days of Aegon the Conquerer, the arms of House Targaryen had borne a three-headed dragon, red on black. Daemon the Pretender had reversed those colors on his own banners, as many bastards did.
why: Origin of the Blackfyre banner-color reversal.

quote: If Aegon the Unworthy had given his sword to his heir Daeron instead of his bastard Daemon, there might never have been a Blackfyre Rebellion, and Roger of Pennytree might be alive today.
why: Dunk's reflection that the sword-gift to Daemon was the rebellion's root cause.

quote: for the Raven's Teeth had gained the top of Weeping Ridge, and Bloodraven saw his half brother's royal standard three hundred yards away, and Daemon and his sons beneath it. He slew Aegon first, the elder of the twins, for he knew that Daemon would never leave the boy whilst warmth lingered in his body, though white shafts fell like rain. Nor did he, though seven arrows pierced him, driven as much by sorcery as by Bloodraven's bow.
why: Bloodraven's decisive shooting of Daemon and his sons from Weeping Ridge.

quote: The singers can go on about their hammer and their anvil, ser, but it was the kinslayer who turned the tide with a white arrow and a black spell. He rules us now as well, make no mistake. King Aerys is his creature.
why: Eustace's claim that Bloodraven's sorcery, not Baelor/Maekar, won the Redgrass Field and now rules Aerys I.

quote: "A great battle is a terrible thing," the old knight said "but in the midst of blood and carnage, there is sometimes also beauty, beauty that could break your heart. I will never forget the way the sun looked when it set upon the Redgrass Field . . . ten thousand men had died, and the air was thick with moans and lamentations, but above us the sky turned gold and red and orange, so beautiful it made me weep to know that my sons would never see it."
why: Eustace's beauty-in-carnage recollection of the sunset over the Redgrass Field.

quote: That was what they always said of those who had perished during the Great Spring Sickness two years past. He died in the spring. Many tens of thousands had died in the spring, among them a wise old king and two young princes full of promise.
why: The Great Spring Sickness claims Rohanne's fourth husband along with the king and two princes.

quote: I thought how you should speak to Lady Webber, ser. You should win her to your side with gallant compliments.
why: Egg coaches Dunk on gallantry before the meeting with Rohanne.

quote: It was the girl he had seen earlier at the archery butts. She had a quiver of arrows on one hip, and held a longbow that was just as tall as she was, which wasn't very tall.
why: Reveal that the young archer, not Lady Helicent, is the Red Widow herself.

quote: He wondered whether he and Egg would be offered hospitality at Coldmoat. A tankard of cool brown ale would go down well. Dunk was considering that prospect with pleasure when he remembered what Egg had said about the Red Widow poisoning her husbands. His thirst fled at once.
why: Poison-fear undercuts Dunk's hope for hospitality at Coldmoat.

quote: "A tree cat. I had not known there were any left in this wood. I wonder what killed him."
why: Eustace notes an unexplained dead tree cat found in Wat's Wood.

quote: 'tis Lord Rivers who rules us, with his spells and spies. There is no one to oppose him. Prince Maekar sulks at Summerhall, nursing his grievances against his royal brother.
why: Septon Sefton names Bloodraven the de facto ruler under the weak King Aerys I.

quote: Aerys keeps his own apartments, and it is said that he would sooner take a book to bed than any woman.
why: King Aerys I's neglect of Queen Aelinor and refusal to sire an heir.

quote: "She struck you?" His mouth opened and closed. "She struck my envoy, who came to her beneath the chequy lion? She dared lay hands upon your person?"
why: Ser Eustace invokes envoy protection under the chequy lion sigil after Rohanne slaps Dunk, a breach of the diplomatic norm.

quote: Bittersteel and the sons of Daemon Blackfyre are hatching plots in Tyrosh, and Dagon Greyjoy's krakens prowl the sunset sea like wolves, raiding as far south as the Arbor.
why: Bittersteel and the Blackfyre exiles regrouping in Tyrosh.

quote: "Chiefly he wanted Coldmoat. His sons paid for his treason with their life's blood. When he brought their bones home and delivered his daughter to the king's men for a hostage, his wife threw herself from the top of Standfast tower. Did Ser Eustace tell you that?"
why: Rohanne's account of Eustace's Blackfyre allegiance and its cost to his sons.

quote: He filled two cups with wine and one with water. The third he gave to Egg, who gave it a long dubious look and put it aside. The septon took no notice. "This is an Arbor vintage," he was telling Dunk. "Very fine, and the poison gives it a special piquance."
why: Arbor wine served to Dunk at Coldmoat under the shadow of the Red Widow's poisoning reputation.

quote: "Don't drink the wine, ser," Egg whispered to him as they waited with the septon in her audience chamber.
why: Egg's poison warning as they wait to be received by the Red Widow.

quote: They piled them in the Dragonpit instead, and when the corpses were ten feet deep, Lord Rivers commanded the pyromancers to burn them. The light of the fires shone through the windows, as it did of yore when living dragons still nested beneath the dome. By night you could see the glow all through the city, the dark green glow of wildfire.
why: Septon Sefton's account of the Great Spring Sickness dead burned with wildfire in the Dragonpit.

quote: He hoped the King's Hand did not have a thousand ears and one as well. Some of what Septon Sefton was saying sounded treasonous. He glanced at Egg, to see how he was taking all of this.
why: Dunk fears Bloodraven's surveillance reaches even Septon Sefton's treasonous gossip.

quote: She loved the boy, and him her. It never went beyond a kiss or two, but . . . it was Addam she wept for after the Redgrass Field, not the husband she hardly knew.
why: Rohanne's love for Addam Osgrey, dead at twelve, root of her enmity with Eustace.

quote: "As the river is called the Mander, though the Manderlys were driven from its banks a thousand years ago. Highgarden is still Highgarden, though the last Gardener died on the Field of Fire. Casterly Rock teems with Lannisters, and nowhere a Casterly to be found. The world changes, ser.
why: Rohanne's argument that names outlast the bloodlines that gave them, justifying her claim to the Chequy Water.

quote: "And so they did. The rightful king, Daemon Blackfyre. The King Who Bore the Sword." The old man's mustache quivered. "The men of the red dragon call themselves the loyalists , but we who chose the black were just as loyal, once.
why: Eustace openly reveals his sons died for Daemon Blackfyre, not King Daeron.

quote: In his heart the black dragon never died, Dunk realized.
why: Dunk recognizes Eustace remains a Blackfyre loyalist decades after the rebellion's end.

quote: "I call it being wroth," Egg declared loftily. "His Grace should have made my father Hand. He's his brother , and the finest battle commander in the realm since Uncle Baelor died.
why: Egg's royal identity is established through his father Prince Maekar, King Aerys's brother.

quote: "If Daemon had ridden over Gwayne Corbray . . . if Fireball had not been slain on the eve of battle . . . if Hightower and Tarbeck and Oakheart and Butterwell had lent us their full strength instead of trying to keep one foot in each camp . . . if Manfred Lothston had proved true instead of treacherous . . . if storms had not delayed Lord Bracken's sailing with the Myrish crossbowmen . . . if Quickfinger had not been caught with the stolen dragon's eggs . . . so many if s, ser . . . had any one come out differently, it could all have turned t'other way.
why: Eustace's catalogue of near-misses that could have won the Blackfyre Rebellion for the black dragon.

quote: Blackfyre, the sword of Aegon the Conquerer, the blade that every Targaryen king had wielded since the Conquest . . . he put that sword in Daemon's hand the day he knighted him, a boy of twelve.
why: Aegon IV gave the ancestral sword Blackfyre to his bastard Daemon rather than his heir Daeron.

quote: Daeron surrounded himself with maesters, septons, and singers. Always there were women whispering in his ear, and his court was full of Dornishmen. How not, when he had taken a Dornishwoman into his bed, and sold his own sweet sister to the prince of Dorne, though it was Daemon that she loved?
why: Eustace's hostile portrait of King Daeron II's Dornish marriage-pact and court.

quote: Daemon Blackfyre, Bittersteel, even Bloodraven. Lord Rivers was more cunning than the other two, he said, but in the end he would prove himself a traitor, too. The High Septon counseled my father never to put any trust in him, nor in any other bastards, great or small.
why: The old High Septon's judgment that Bloodraven, like the other Great Bastards, would prove a traitor.

quote: Though now . . . all the men who marched beside me to seat Prince Daemon on the Iron Throne have melted away like morning dew. Mayhaps I dreamed them. Or more like, Lord Bloodraven and his Raven's Teeth have put the fear in them.
why: Eustace credits Bloodraven's Raven's Teeth with terrorizing surviving Blackfyre men into silence.

quote: I bought my head back with my daughter's life. Alysanne was seven when they took her off to King's Landing and twenty when she died, a silent sister. I went to King's Landing once to see her, and she would not even speak to me, her own father. A king's mercy is a poisoned gift.
why: The hostage-price of Eustace's pardon: his daughter Alysanne, sent to King's Landing and later a silent sister.

quote: Dunk need only lift his eyes to find familiar friends: the Stallion and the Sow, the King's Crown and the Crone's Lantern, the Galley, Ghost, and Moonmaid. But there were clouds to the north, and the blue eye of the Ice Dragon was lost to him, the blue eye that pointed north.
why: The Ice Dragon constellation and its north-pointing blue eye, obscured by smoke.

quote: The last of the green kings perished on that field, with the finest flowers of the Reach around him. My father said the dragonfire burned so hot that their swords melted in their hands.
why: Eustace's account of the Field of Fire as the origin of House Osgrey's decline.

quote: Dunk's head was full of dragons, red and black . . . full of chequy lions, old shields, battered boots . . . full of streams and moats and dams, and papers stamped with the king's great seal that he could not read.
why: Dunk's restless dream-inventory folding together the novella's motifs before the trial by battle.

quote: Only then did Lady Rohanne herself appear, astride a coal-black mare decked out in strands of silverly silk, like unto a spider's web. The Widow's cloak was made of the same stuff. It billowed from her shoulders and her wrists, as light as air.
why: Rohanne's spider-web-and-scale entrance at the stream for the trial by battle.

quote: "A signet. Gold and onyx." Her green eyes narrowed as she studied the seal. "Where did you find this, ser?" "In a boot. Wrapped in rags and stuffed up in the toe."
why: Dunk shows Rohanne the hidden Targaryen signet ring, gesturing at Egg's concealed identity without naming him.

quote: "You are quite mad." The smoke had filled her eyes with tears. "If you were better born, I'd marry you."
why: Rohanne's half-serious marriage line to Dunk after he cuts his own cheek.

quote: Every day I wake wondering if this might be the day he marries me by force." Her hand curled tight around her braid, as hard as if it were a rope, and she was dangling over a precipice. "He wants to, I know. He holds back for fear of my wroth, just as Conklyn and Stackhouse and the Durwells tread carefully where the Red Widow is concerned.
why: Rohanne's fear that her castellan Lucas Inchfield will marry her by force.

quote: "Please reconsider, ser. These are perilous times, even for dragons and their friends. Stay until you've healed."
why: Rohanne obliquely acknowledges Egg's dragon nature while urging Dunk to stay.

quote: And Egg can join my other squires. No one need ever know who he is.
why: Rohanne offers to shelter Egg's concealed identity among her own squires.

quote: Dunk went to one knee beside Egg, and put the signet back in the boy's hand; four three-headed dragons, two and two, the arms of Maekar, Prince of Summerhall.
why: Maekar's differenced Targaryen arms, borne on the signet ring hidden in Egg's boot.

quote: Lord Wyman and Ser Eustace were fast friends, until the Blackfyre Rebellion.
why: The Blackfyre Rebellion split a Webber-Osgrey friendship along the loyalist-rebel fault line.

quote: Count yourself fortunate that I am ironborn. The priests of the Drowned God know how to drown a man and bring him back, and I have made a study of their beliefs and customs.
why: Maester Cerrick's ironborn Drowned God expertise, used to revive the drowned Dunk.

quote: His lordship and my lady were wed this morning, by our good Septon Sefton. Eustace Osgrey is the lord of Coldmoat, and his chequy lion flies beside the Webber spider on every tower and wall.
why: The Osgrey-Webber marriage merges the feuding houses; the chequy lion flies beside the spider.

quote: "I took something else to remember her by instead. A lock of that red hair." He reached under his cloak, brought out the braid, and smiled.
why: Dunk cuts off a lock of Rohanne's braid as a keepsake, refusing her gift horse.

quote: "The Wall is north."
why: At the crossroads, Egg points Dunk north toward the Wall instead of south to Summerhall.
